\[
\frac{5}{9}  -0.67  \sqrt{123}  \sqrt{49}  3.142
\]

(h)
\begin{subpartsaliged}

Antonio buys a restaurant for \$240\,000.\\
This is $\frac{5}{8}$ of the amount he has available to spend.

(a) Show that he has \$144\,000 left after buying the restaurant.

(b) Some of the \$144\,000 is spent on expenses.
Expenses are wages, equipment and supplies in the ratio

\[
\text{wages} : \text{equipment} : \text{supplies} = 9 : 5 : 8.
\]

The amount spent on wages is \$45\,000.

(i) (c) Find the amount spent on

\begin{subsubparts}

(d) equipment

(e) supplies.

\end{subsubparts}

(f) Work out the amount Antonio has left now.

(g) Antonio borrows \$25\,400 for 6 years at a rate of 5\% per year simple interest.

Calculate the total amount he repays at the end of the 6 years.

(h) In one week, the number of customers in the restaurant was 560.
In the next week, the number of customers in the restaurant was 656.

Calculate the percentage increase.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline

\hline
$y$ & & $1$ & & $7$ & & & & $-5$ \\

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph.

(g) Write down the coordinates of the two points where the two graphs intersect.
texHtml:
(a) Complete the table of values for $y = 5 + 3x - x^2$.
$x$$-2$$-1$$0$$1$$2$$3$$4$$5$
$y$$1$$7$$-5$
(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 5 + 3x - x^2$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$. (c) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph. (d) (i) (e) Complete the table of values for $y = 2x + 1$.
$x$$-1$$0$$2$
$y$
(f) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + 1$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$. (g) Write down the coordinates of the two points where the two graphs intersect.

tex:
Two friends, Diego and Javier, meet at a swimming pool.
The travel graph shows Diego's journey by bicycle from his home to the swimming pool.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Calculate Diego's speed for his journey from his home to the swimming pool.
Give your answer in kilometres per hour.

(b) Diego stays at the swimming pool until 12 20.

(i) (c) On the grid, draw the line representing the time he stays at the swimming pool.

(d) Work out how long, in hours and minutes, he is at the swimming pool.

(e) Javier leaves his home 15 minutes later than Diego.
He walks to the swimming pool at a constant speed of 6 km/h.

On the grid, show Javier's journey from his home to the swimming pool.

(f) They both leave the swimming pool at 12 20 and return to their own homes, each at a constant speed.
Diego arrives home at 12 45.
Javier arrives home 5 minutes later than Diego.

Complete the travel graph.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a right-angled triangle, $ABC$, and a semicircle.
The radius of the semicircle is 4.5 cm.
$AC = 8.9$ cm and $BC = 13.2$ cm.

(a) Calculate the shaded area.
Give the units of your answer.

(b) Calculate $AB$.
